summary: The review of the Student Research Agreement reveals a generally low-risk posture, with most clauses flagged as blue for falling outside the scope of University of Auckland (UoA) Preferred Contracting Positions or Standard Templates. However, several red and amber flags highlight critical issues, including ambiguous publication rights, indefinite embargoes on theses, and unclear intellectual property provisions, which deviate from UoA's policies. Immediate referral to Auckland UniServices Ltd for IP clauses and escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts for publication and dispute resolution concerns is recommended to ensure compliance and alignment with UoA standards.

flags.clause_title: 6. The Research is to be primarily undertaken at the
flags.snippet: 6. The Research is to be primarily undertaken at the Confidential Information means any information relating Location. to the business or financial affairs of the University, Obligations of the Student the existence a
flags.rationale: The clause text appears to conflate the location of research with the definition of 'Confidential Information,' which is a critical topic under UoA Position #POS-11. The clause does not clearly align with the Preferred or Acceptable tier for confidentiality definitions, and the wording is ambiguous and incomplete. This requires escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts for clarification and potential redrafting to ensure compliance with UoA's confidentiality standards.

flags.clause_title: 14. The Stipend (if any) and the Fees (if any) shall be paid as
flags.snippet: 14. The Stipend (if any) and the Fees (if any) shall be paid as embargo of the Thesis will be reviewed every two years set out in the Schedule. and will cease where it is no longer reasonable to require continued embargo.
flags.rationale: The clause addresses the embargo of a thesis, which directly impacts the UoA-sensitive topic of Publication. According to UoA Position #POS-12, students must retain publication rights for their thesis or academic publication, including commercially sensitive content. While a stand-down period of up to 12 months is acceptable, this clause introduces a review every two years and does not specify a maximum embargo duration, which could lead to an indefinite embargo. This materially deviates from the Preferred and Acceptable tiers and requires escalation to the School of Graduate Studies for review and approval.

flags.clause_title: 17. Subject to clause 29, the Student and the University agree
flags.snippet: 17. Subject to clause 29, the Student and the University agree Publication on the Research is produced by the Student. to sign all documents and do all things reasonably
flags.rationale: The clause touches on the sensitive topic of Publication but does not align with UoA's Preferred or Acceptable positions. It lacks clarity on the Principal Investigator's right to publish, reasonable patent stand-down periods, and the student's publication rights for thesis or academic work. Additionally, the clause does not address embargo periods or commercially sensitive content, which are critical considerations under UoA Position #POS-12. Escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts is required to ensure compliance with UoA's publication policies.

flags.clause_title: 19. The Parties will keep confidential, and will not use, or
flags.snippet: 19. The Parties will keep confidential, and will not use, or alternations or deletions to the Publication as permit to be used, any Confidential Information that is requested by prior to submitting the Publication. disc…
flags.rationale: The clause text is unclear and appears to conflate confidentiality obligations with publication rights, creating ambiguity. It does not align with UoA's Preferred Position #POS-11, which requires a clear definition of confidential information, exclusions for public or independently developed information, and the ability for UoA to disclose to agents and Affiliates. Additionally, the clause does not specify a duration for confidentiality obligations, which is a critical requirement. The clause must be escalated to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts for clarification and potential redrafting.

flags.clause_title: 20. The Parties shall continue to keep the Confidential
flags.snippet: 20. The Parties shall continue to keep the Confidential Student by name where the Student has contributed Information confidential following the expiry or termination materially to the research being published.
flags.rationale: The clause deviates materially from UoA Position #POS-11 on Confidentiality. It does not specify a time limit for confidentiality obligations (e.g., 5–7 years post-termination/expiry), which is required under both Preferred and Acceptable tiers. Additionally, the clause introduces ambiguity by referencing the Student's name in connection with confidentiality, which is not addressed in the UoA Position. This requires escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts for review and potential amendment.

The clause appears to touch on confidentiality obligations but does not clearly define exclusions (e.g., public knowledge, independently developed information, etc.) or address disclosure to agents and Affiliates. Additionally, the clause introduces termination rights without clarity on accrued rights or obligations, which may conflict with UoA's Preferred termination position (POS-19). Escalation to Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts is required for both confidentiality and termination concerns.

flags.clause_title: 26. Any changes to the Thesis reasonably required by
flags.snippet: 26. Any changes to the Thesis reasonably required by
flags.rationale: The clause text is incomplete and ambiguous, making it impossible to determine alignment with UoA's Preferred or Acceptable positions on Publication. Specifically, UoA Position #POS-12 requires that students retain publication rights for their thesis or academic publications, including commercially sensitive content, and any embargo must not exceed 12 months. The clause as written does not clarify who can require changes, under what conditions, or whether these changes could infringe on the student's publication rights. This ambiguity triggers escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts for clarification and potential revision.

flags.clause_title: 28. Where publication could potentially damage
flags.snippet: 28. Where publication could potentially damage $1,000,000. commercial interests, or prevent from protecting any
flags.rationale: The clause text is incomplete and ambiguous, but it appears to impose restrictions on publication that could damage commercial interests or prevent protection of intellectual property. This materially deviates from UoA's Preferred Position, which ensures the Principal Investigator and students retain publication rights, with reasonable stand-down periods for patent protection (no more than 60 days) and embargoes on commercially sensitive content limited to 12 months. The clause does not align with either the Preferred or Acceptable tier and raises concerns about excessive restrictions on academic freedom and student publication rights. Escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts is required.

flags.clause_title: 34. may terminate this Agreement by giving fourteen (14)
flags.snippet: 34. may terminate this Agreement by giving fourteen (14)
flags.rationale: The clause text suggests termination for convenience with 14 days' notice, which is not aligned with UoA's Preferred Position for Termination (POS-19). UoA's Preferred Position requires mutual two-way termination for material breach or insolvency, with specific provisions for termination costs and accrued rights. Termination for convenience is only acceptable for Consulting contracts under strict conditions, which do not apply to Student Research Agreements. This clause materially deviates from the Preferred Position and requires escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts.

Material breach clauses are addressed under POS-19 (Termination), which specifies mutual two-way termination for material breach with clear accrued rights and termination cost provisions. This clause must be escalated to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts for clarification and completion.

flags.rationale: The clause text is incomplete and does not clearly align with UoA Position #POS-07 on Liability Limitations and Exclusions. It fails to specify a monetary cap, does not exclude consequential or indirect losses, and does not address liability for loss of data, profit, revenue, savings, business, or goodwill. Additionally, the clause appears to conflate liability limitations with unrelated procedural requirements (e.g., thesis submission). This requires escalation to the Research Grants and Contracts Delegations for clarification and potential redrafting.

flags.rationale: This clause raises significant concerns as it combines unrelated topics (insurance and publication) and introduces the possibility of an indefinite embargo on the thesis. This materially deviates from UoA Position #POS-12, which allows a maximum embargo of 12 months for student-produced commercially sensitive content. Indefinite embargoes are explicitly disallowed, as they conflict with the student's academic rights and the University's publication policies. Additionally, the clause's reference to insurance endorsement for Intellectual Property rights is ambiguous and does not align with UoA's Preferred or Acceptable positions on insurance (POS-09). Escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts and the School of Graduate Studies is required.

flags.clause_title: 40. No Party shall be responsble to the other Parties for any
flags.snippet: 40. No Party shall be responsble to the other Parties for any property, confidentiality, publication, termination and delay or failure in performance of any obligations imposed governing law, shall not expire when this Agreement ends.
flags.rationale: The clause is poorly drafted and ambiguous, making it difficult to determine its alignment with UoA's Preferred or Acceptable positions. It appears to conflate multiple topics (property, confidentiality, publication, termination, governing law, and force majeure) without clear structure or intent. Governing Law and Jurisdiction is a sensitive topic, and the clause does not explicitly state the governing law or jurisdiction, which is required to align with UoA Position #POS-16. Additionally, the clause's reference to obligations not expiring when the Agreement ends is vague and could conflict with termination provisions. Escalation to General Counsel is required for clarification and redrafting.

flags.clause_title: 41. If any dispute arises between the Parties in relation to this
flags.snippet: 41. If any dispute arises between the Parties in relation to this
flags.rationale: The clause text is incomplete and does not provide sufficient detail to confirm alignment with UoA Position #POS-18 on Dispute Resolution. The Preferred Position requires good faith discussions, escalation to senior management, mediation before arbitration/litigation, and allowance for urgent interlocutory action through courts. The clause as written does not address any of these elements, nor does it provide clarity on the dispute resolution process. This deviation triggers escalation to the Senior Manager Research Grants and Contracts or General Counsel for review and amendment.

flags.clause_title: 50. Any variations, waivers and modifications of this
flags.snippet: 50. Any variations, waivers and modifications of this Agreement, then the Parties will negotiate promptly in Agreement, shall only be valid if in writing and signed by good faith in order to amicably resolve the dispute themselves.
flags.rationale: This clause conflates two distinct topics: variations/modifications and dispute resolution. While variations must be in writing and signed, the inclusion of 'negotiate promptly in good faith to amicably resolve the dispute' introduces ambiguity and overlaps with the dispute resolution protocol. The clause does not align with UoA Position #POS-18, which requires escalation to senior management followed by mediation before arbitration/litigation, and explicitly allows urgent interlocutory action through courts. Escalation to Senior Manager Research Grants and Contracts or General Counsel is required to ensure compliance with UoA's dispute resolution standards.

flags.rationale: This clause touches on the sensitive topic of Publication and potentially Intellectual Property. While the clause mentions granting a royalty-free, non-exclusive right to reproduce the thesis and inventions, it is unclear whether this aligns with UoA's Preferred Position on publication rights for students and Principal Investigators. Specifically, UoA requires that students retain publication rights for their thesis or academic publications, including commercially sensitive content, and that any embargo on publication should not exceed 12 months. Additionally, the clause may intersect with Intellectual Property considerations, which must be referred to Auckland UniServices Ltd for review. The clause does not explicitly violate UoA's Acceptable tier but requires confirmation and potential escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts for publication concerns and to UniServices for IP concerns.

flags.clause_title: 12. The Student will keep accurate and systematic records
flags.snippet: 12. The Student will keep accurate and systematic records the Student as detailed in the Schedule (if any). and provide progress reports relating to the Research as reasonably requested by the Supervisor. “Thesis” means the Student’s thesis.
flags.rationale: This clause pertains to the Student's obligation to maintain accurate records and provide progress reports, which aligns with standard academic practices and does not restrict the Student's publication rights for their thesis or academic publications. The clause does not impose any embargo or stand-down period that would conflict with UoA's Preferred or Acceptable positions on publication rights. Therefore, it matches the Preferred tier under UoA Position #POS-12.

the Student as detailed in the Schedule (if any).
and provide progress reports relating to the Research as
reasonably requested by the Supervisor.
“Thesis” means the Student’s thesis.

flags.clause_title: 25. undertakes to review the Thesis within twenty (20)
flags.snippet: 25. undertakes to review the Thesis within twenty (20) circumstances; or Working Days of receiving the Thesis.
flags.rationale: The clause aligns with UoA Position #POS-12 on Publication, as it supports the timely review of a thesis, which is critical for students to retain their publication rights and fulfill academic requirements. The 20 Working Days timeframe is reasonable and does not impose an embargo or delay that would conflict with the Preferred or Acceptable tiers.

flags.clause_title: 29. The Student shall retain copyright in the Thesis, provided
flags.snippet: 29. The Student shall retain copyright in the Thesis, provided
flags.rationale: This clause aligns with UoA Position #POS-13 on Moral Rights, which explicitly states that moral rights (including copyright) are never waived for thesis or academic publications. The clause supports the student's retention of copyright in their thesis, which is consistent with the preferred tier of UoA's policy.

flags.rationale: This clause aligns with UoA's Preferred Position for Governing Law and Jurisdiction (POS-16), which specifies New Zealand as the governing law and jurisdiction. The clause explicitly states that the parties will not object to the jurisdiction of New Zealand courts, which matches the Preferred tier. Additionally, the clause confirms the independent contractor status of the Student, which is consistent with the UoA template and does not create an employment relationship.

flags.rationale: This clause aligns with UoA Position #POS-18 on Dispute Resolution, which explicitly allows urgent interlocutory action through courts. The clause does not conflict with the Governing Law and Jurisdiction position (#POS-16) as it does not specify jurisdiction but rather preserves the right to seek urgent relief. This is consistent with the Preferred tier for dispute resolution.
